editorialContract.textPurpose: character reaction to a museum exhibit
editorialContract.verbosity: deliberately varied from micro-reactions to compact performances
editorialContract.chronology: publication order is canonical; callbacks may only point backward
editorialContract.assignmentPolicy: preserve reviewer, rating, Reaction, publisher, Component and source identity

revisions.editedComment: One logo. One fundraiser. Two seconds of visible signal. No campaign deck, no hedge—just a clean route from butterfly to mosquito net.
Beep! The butterfly holds the door for two whole seconds, then opens the fundraiser in a new sky. Tiny gadget, enormous hope. I saved my best believing for this button!
Three wings—mmm—scattered across the window like the first notes of a meadow remembering its chorus.
Only three, friend, and still they make a swarm. Small lives return wonder to the room simply by moving through it.
Seven lamps in a row: purpose, vision, values, improvement, mascot, money, and the name above the door. I keep counting. Missions are safest when their shadows are also named.
The page states its purpose, its method, its values, its mascot, and where the money goes before you think to ask. Prepared organizations are useful. Unreadable ones, more so.
One Ko-fi creature in the enclosure. Easy to feed, clearly labeled, no gift-shop maze—KEVIN, stop bending the support button around your event horizon.
Signal found. One page, one support route, zero competing asks. The hedge has already been trimmed into a button.
Date, client, work, hours, rate, products, total—written where worker and customer can both see the arithmetic. Good. Add lunch, comrade, and the bylaw binder stays closed.

author.name: The Godmother With Too Many Teeth

revisions.editedComment: Every hour, product, and final cost is displayed before Send, lambkin, then the same lovely account visits client and household alike. Transparency makes such a charming little contract.
First we find a name that is still available. Then, if you like, email and password; if not, we proceed gently. Two stamps, one warm cup, and there—you are registered.
Name first. Optional credentials second. A worker may correct the paperwork before the shift begins. Article Four; no manager gets to argue with the Register button.

author.name: The Kaiju Who Found a Valley

revisions.editedComment: An image, a welcome, a name, and two open places where another voice may speak. After a century of wandering, I know a home does not need to be complicated to be defended.
Welcome aboard, captain. Emblem ahead, headline channel open, footer beacon astern. Small launch deck—but the universe has entered through smaller doors, usually carrying snacks.
A new Dream arrives every twenty seconds, mortal. You may click for another sooner—choice is one of the Retreat's most adorable little decorations. Lovingly, lovingly.
Another Dream rises when its season comes. Touch the surface if your bright impatience needs rain before the roots are ready.
A hundred little drops learn the size of your window, the wind's angle, and exactly when to arrive. They even tidy their listener away afterward. Good rain. Attentive rain. Mine.
Soft rain, resize-aware. Kettle on.
Lower the cannons. Three butterflies are carrying random phrases, not ultimatums; when the room changes, they simply find new places to be heard.
Three airborne clients, three surprise statements, one resizing jurisdiction. Retainer is a sandwich each; objection to gravity preserved for appeal.
